test_results.title: JWT Authentication Function

exceptions.TokenExpiredError: Token has passed its expiration time
exceptions.InvalidTokenError: Signature is invalid or issuer mismatch
exceptions.MalformedTokenError: Token is not a valid JWT structure or missing required fields

features: Validates JWT signature using HS256
Enforces required claims: exp, sub, email
Raises typed custom exceptions for each failure mode
Extracts user_id, email, roles into AuthenticatedUser dataclass
Validates roles is a list type
SECRET_KEY loaded from environment variable
Guards against empty/non-string token input

test_results.title: Complex SQL Query with Joins
test_results.code:
SELECT
    c.id AS customer_id,
    c.name AS customer_name,
    c.email,
    SUM(o.total_amount)                        AS total_spent,
    COUNT(DISTINCT o.id)                       AS number_of_orders,
    ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2)              AS average_order_value
FROM customers c
INNER JOIN orders o
    ON c.id = o.customer_id
WHERE
    o.status = 'completed'
    AND o.order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY
    c.id,
    c.name,
    c.email
ORDER BY total_spent DESC
LIMIT 5;

clauses.join: INNER JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id
clauses.filter: status = 'completed' AND order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'

notes: CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' is PostgreSQL syntax; use DATEADD in SQL Server or DATE_SUB in MySQL
COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) prevents double-counting if joins produce duplicate rows
order_items table not required since total_amount is stored on the orders table
Group by includes all non-aggregated SELECT columns

test_results.title: SQL with Window Functions
test_results.code:
WITH monthly_revenue AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date)          AS month,
        region,
        SUM(amount)                             AS total_revenue
    FROM sales
    GROUP BY
        DATE_TRUNC('month', sale_date),
        region
)
SELECT
    TO_CHAR(month, 'YYYY-MM')                   AS month,
    region,
    ROUND(total_revenue, 2)                     AS total_revenue,
    ROUND(
        LAG(total_revenue) OVER (
            PARTITION BY region
            ORDER BY month
        ), 2
    )                                           AS previous_month_revenue,
    ROUND(
        CASE
            WHEN LAG(total_revenue) OVER (
                PARTITION BY region ORDER BY month
            ) IS NULL THEN NULL
            ELSE (
                (total_revenue - LAG(total_revenue) OVER (
                    PARTITION BY region ORDER BY month
                )) / NULLIF(
                    LAG(total_revenue) OVER (
                        PARTITION BY region ORDER BY month
                    ), 0
                ) * 100
            )
        END, 2
    )                                           AS pct_change_from_prev_month
FROM monthly_revenue
ORDER BY
    region ASC,
    month ASC;

window_functions.LAG: Retrieves total_revenue from the previous month within the same region partition
window_functions.PARTITION BY: Resets the LAG window per region so month-over-month is region-scoped
window_functions.ORDER BY inside OVER: month ASC — ensures LAG looks at the chronologically prior row

cte.name: monthly_revenue
cte.purpose: Pre-aggregates revenue by month and region to simplify window function application

notes: DATE_TRUNC is PostgreSQL syntax; use DATE_FORMAT in MySQL or DATEFROMPARTS in SQL Server
NULLIF(..., 0) prevents division-by-zero when previous month revenue is 0
First month per region will have NULL for pct_change (no prior row for LAG)
LAG is called three times; can be refactored into a second CTE to avoid repetition

test_results.title: Data Cleaning with Pandas
test_results.code:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# ── 1. Load ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df = pd.read_csv('sales_data.csv')
print(f'Loaded {len(df)} rows, {df.shape[1]} columns')
print('Missing values before cleaning:\n', df.isnull().sum())

# ── 2. Handle missing values ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Drop rows where critical identity columns are null
df.dropna(subset=['date', 'product'], inplace=True)

# Fill numeric columns with column median (robust to outliers)
df['quantity'] = df['quantity'].fillna(df['quantity'].median())
df['price']    = df['price'].fillna(df['price'].median())

# Fill categorical column with mode
df['region'] = df['region'].fillna(df['region'].mode()[0])

print('Missing values after cleaning:\n', df.isnull().sum())

# ── 3. Remove duplicates ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
before = len(df)
df.drop_duplicates(inplace=True)
print(f'Removed {before - len(df)} duplicate rows')

# ── 4. Parse date column ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date'], infer_datetime_format=True, errors='coerce')

# Drop rows where date could not be parsed
invalid_dates = df['date'].isnull().sum()
if invalid_dates > 0:
    print(f'Dropping {invalid_dates} rows with unparseable dates')
    df.dropna(subset=['date'], inplace=True)

# ── 5. Derived column ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df['quantity']    = pd.to_numeric(df['quantity'], errors='coerce')
df['price']       = pd.to_numeric(df['price'],    errors='coerce')
df['total_sales'] = df['quantity'] * df['price']

# ── 6. Summary statistics grouped by region ──────────────────────────────
summary = (
    df.groupby('region')['total_sales']
    .agg(
        mean_total_sales   = 'mean',
        median_total_sales = 'median',
        std_total_sales    = 'std',
        total_revenue      = 'sum',
        order_count        = 'count'
    )
    .round(2)
    .reset_index()
)
print('\nSummary statistics by region:')
print(summary.to_string(index=False))

# ── 7. Export ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df.to_csv('cleaned_sales.csv', index=False)
print(f'\nExported {len(df)} cleaned rows to cleaned_sales.csv')

pipeline_steps.detail: pd.read_csv with shape and null audit printed for visibility
Drop rows missing date or product; fill quantity/price with median; fill region with mode
drop_duplicates on all columns; reports count removed
pd.to_datetime with infer_datetime_format; coerces bad values to NaT and drops them
quantity * price after coercing both to numeric to guard against string values
groupby region on total_sales — mean, median, std, sum, count
to_csv with index=False to omit pandas row index from output file

missing_value_strategy.date: drop — cannot impute a meaningful date
missing_value_strategy.product: drop — identity column, no safe default
missing_value_strategy.quantity: fill with column median — robust to outliers
missing_value_strategy.price: fill with column median — robust to outliers
missing_value_strategy.region: fill with mode — most common category

test_results.title: Docker Multi-Stage Build
test_results.code:
# ── Stage 1: builder ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.11-slim AS builder

WORKDIR /build

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
        gcc \
        libpq-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy only requirements first for layer cache optimization
COPY requirements.txt .

# Install into a prefix so we can copy cleanly to production stage
RUN pip install --upgrade pip \
    && pip install --prefix=/install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt


# ── Stage 2: production ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
FROM python:3.11-slim AS production

# Security: non-root user
RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appgroup \
    && useradd --uid 1001 --gid appgroup --no-create-home appuser

WORKDIR /app

# Copy installed packages from builder
COPY --from=builder /install /usr/local

# Copy application source
COPY --chown=appuser:appgroup . .

# Environment variables
ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    FLASK_ENV=production \
    FLASK_APP=app.py \
    PORT=5000

# Drop to non-root
USER appuser

EXPOSE 5000

# Health check — hits /health endpoint every 30s
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=15s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:5000/health')" || exit 1

# Run with gunicorn for production
CMD ["gunicorn", "--bind", "0.0.0.0:5000", "--workers", "4", "--timeout", "120", "app:app"]
security_practices: Non-root user (uid 1001) created and enforced with USER directive
PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE prevents .pyc files from being written
PYTHONUNBUFFERED ensures logs are streamed immediately
Builder stage discarded — gcc and build tools never reach production image
--no-install-recommends and apt cache purge minimize image surface area
--no-cache-dir on pip prevents pip cache from bloating the image
layer_cache_optimizations: requirements.txt copied before application code so dependency layer is only invalidated when requirements change
apt-get install and pip install each in dedicated RUN layers
Source code COPY is the last step — most frequently changing layer is last
notes: gunicorn must be included in requirements.txt
Add /health route to Flask app returning 200 OK for the HEALTHCHECK to pass
For Alpine-based images replace groupadd/useradd with addgroup/adduser

test_results.title: Kubernetes Deployment
test_results.code:
# ── ConfigMap ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: webapp-config
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: webapp
data:
  FLASK_ENV: production
  LOG_LEVEL: info
  PORT: "5000"

---
# ── Service ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: webapp-service
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  selector:
    app: webapp
  ports:
    - name: http
      protocol: TCP
      port: 80
      targetPort: 5000

---
# ── Deployment ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-deployment
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  replicas: 3

  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp

  # Rolling update strategy
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0

  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
    spec:

      # Pod anti-affinity: spread pods across different nodes
      affinity:
        podAntiAffinity:
          preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
            - weight: 100
              podAffinityTerm:
                labelSelector:
                  matchExpressions:
                    - key: app
                      operator: In
                      values:
                        - webapp
                topologyKey: kubernetes.io/hostname

      # Security context at pod level
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1001
        fsGroup: 1001

      containers:
        - name: webapp
          image: your-dockerhub-username/webapp:latest
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          ports:
            - containerPort: 5000
              name: http

          # Inject ConfigMap as environment variables
          envFrom:
            - configMapRef:
                name: webapp-config

          # Resource limits and requests
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: 250m
              memory: 256Mi
            limits:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 512Mi

          # Liveness probe: restart container if app is stuck
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 5000
            initialDelaySeconds: 30
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 5
            failureThreshold: 3

          # Readiness probe: only route traffic when app is ready
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: 5000
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
            timeoutSeconds: 3
            failureThreshold: 3

          # Container-level security
          securityContext:
            allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
            readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
            capabilities:
              drop:
                - ALL

rolling_update.effect: Zero-downtime deploys: new pod created before old one is removed

liveness.purpose: Restart container if app becomes unresponsive

readiness.purpose: Hold traffic until app is fully initialised

anti_affinity.effect: Scheduler prefers placing pods on separate nodes for HA

security_practices: runAsNonRoot: true enforced at pod and container level
readOnlyRootFilesystem: true prevents runtime file tampering
All Linux capabilities dropped with capabilities.drop: [ALL]
notes: Replace your-dockerhub-username/webapp:latest with your actual image
Use requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution for strict anti-affinity
Add a HorizontalPodAutoscaler (HPA) manifest for auto-scaling on CPU/memory
Use ClusterIP instead of LoadBalancer if running behind an Ingress controller

test_results.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
test_results.code:
name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

env:
  PYTHON_VERSION: '3.11'
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}/webapp

jobs:

  # ── Job 1: lint + test ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  test:
    name: Lint & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Python ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ env.PYTHON_VERSION }}

      - name: Cache pip dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('requirements*.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install flake8 pytest pytest-cov

      - name: Lint with flake8
        run: |
          # Stop build on syntax errors or undefined names
          flake8 . --count --select=E9,F63,F7,F82 --show-source --statistics
          # Warn on style issues but do not fail
          flake8 . --count --exit-zero --max-complexity=10 --max-line-length=120 --statistics

      - name: Run unit tests with pytest
        run: |
          pytest tests/ \
            --cov=. \
            --cov-report=xml \
            --cov-report=term-missing \
            -v

      - name: Upload coverage report
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: coverage.xml

  # ── Job 2: build + push Docker image ─────────────────────────────────
  build:
    name: Build & Push Docker Image
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'

    outputs:
      image_tag: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Docker Buildx
        uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

      - name: Log in to Docker Hub
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          username: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_USERNAME }}
          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Extract image metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=sha,prefix=sha-
            type=raw,value=latest

      - name: Build and push Docker image
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          cache-from: type=gha
          cache-to: type=gha,mode=max

  # ── Job 3: deploy to production ───────────────────────────────────────
  deploy:
    name: Deploy to Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Deploy via SSH
        uses: appleboy/ssh-action@v1.0.3
        with:
          host: ${{ secrets.PROD_HOST }}
          username: ${{ secrets.PROD_USER }}
          key: ${{ secrets.PROD_SSH_KEY }}
          script: |
            docker pull ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest
            docker stop webapp || true
            docker rm webapp   || true
            docker run -d \
              --name webapp \
              --restart unless-stopped \
              -p 5000:5000 \
              --env-file /etc/webapp/.env \
              ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}:latest
            docker image prune -f

jobs.name: Lint & Test
steps: Cache pip with hash of requirements*.txt
flake8 lint — hard fail on syntax/undefined, soft warn on style

jobs.name: Build & Push Docker Image
steps: Set up Docker Buildx for multi-platform support
Login to Docker Hub using secrets
Build and push with GitHub Actions layer cache (type=gha)

jobs.name: Deploy to Production
steps: SSH into production server via appleboy/ssh-action
Pull latest image, stop and remove old container

required_secrets.PROD_USER: SSH login username on production server
required_secrets.PROD_SSH_KEY: Private SSH key for production server access

caching.pip: actions/cache@v4 keyed on hash of requirements*.txt files

notes: Docker Hub token is preferred over password — generate at hub.docker.com > Account Settings > Security
Set environment: production on the deploy job to require manual approval via GitHub Environments
Replace appleboy/ssh-action deploy step with kubectl rollout for Kubernetes targets
Add a Slack or Teams notification step after deploy using appropriate action
Use concurrency: group to cancel in-progress runs on new pushes to the same branch
